techniques.evidence_quote: Google dorks revealed Natures basket's 'gnbdevcdn' open bucket
An audit user account was set-up and access to the console was verified.
Second gathered credentials found with root user access: GotRoot!!
Server backend code -> Hardcoded AWS keys in configuration file -> GotRoot!
Gathered credentials were enumerated for permissions. First user account 'gnbrobosoft' found with limited access.
gnbdevcdn bucket was found to be world readable. A quick search revealed hardcoded credentials and AWS access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker used Google dorks to discover the publicly exposed 'gnbdevcdn' S3 bucket belonging to Nature's Basket.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Google dorks revealed Natures basket's 'gnbdevcdn' open bucket

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed the world-readable S3 bucket and browsed its contents, locating server backend configuration files containing hardcoded AWS access keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: gnbdevcdn bucket was found to be world readable. A quick search revealed hardcoded credentials and AWS access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker extracted hardcoded AWS access keys embedded in server-side configuration files retrieved from the open S3 bucket.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Server backend code -> Hardcoded AWS keys in configuration file -> GotRoot!

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the first set of gathered credentials (IAM user 'gnbrobosoft') to enumerate account permissions and discover accessible cloud services.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Gathered credentials were enumerated for permissions. First user account 'gnbrobosoft' found with limited access.

attack_chain.description: Attacker leveraged a second set of hardcoded credentials which turned out to belong to the AWS root account, achieving full account takeover.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Second gathered credentials found with root user access: GotRoot!!

attack_chain.description: Using root account access, an 'audit' IAM user account was created and console access was verified, demonstrating persistent access to the environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: An audit user account was set-up and access to the console was verified.

initial_access_vector: World-readable public S3 bucket ('gnbdevcdn') containing server backend source code with hardcoded AWS root access keys in configuration files, discovered via Google dorking
persistence_mechanism: Created a new IAM 'audit' user account with console access using the compromised root credentials
privilege_escalation: Hardcoded root account access keys found directly in configuration files within the public S3 bucket, bypassing any need for privilege escalation steps — direct root access from the start
